cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. The question is about the order of places from east to west. The options are combinations of San Francisco, Reno, New York, and Columbus. The text describes an imaginary road from San Francisco to New York, lined with businesses. First, I need to remember where these cities are located in the US. San Francisco is on the West Coast, in California. Reno is in Nevada, which is east of California. Columbus is in Ohio, and New York is on the East Coast, specifically New York City. The passage mentions that from San Francisco to about Reno, the flagpoles are small. Then from Reno eastward, they increase in height until Columbus, Ohio, which is about four-fifths of the way across the nation. Then as they approach the eastern terminus (New York), the poles get even taller. So the order from west to east (San Francisco to New York) would be: San Francisco, Reno, Columbus, New York. That would make the correct answer (B) San Francisco, Reno, Columbus, New York. Option (A) has New York before Columbus, which is wrong because
